messageHistory.content: 😊 Sorry, my brain is warming up... Take your time.

😊 **Spring** is a season of the year when most biological life awakens from its winter sleep, thanks to warmer temperatures that melt away snow and ice in many regions across Earth's northern hemisphere. It often brings rain showers as well—which helps plants grow! The months associated with spring are March through May (in the Northern Hemisphere). During this season, you might see flowers blooming everywhere; trees getting new leaves to start their growth cycle for another year; and animals coming out of hibernation or migrating back from warmer regions. Remember that Earth experiences seasons because it's tilted on its axis—not because it's closer or further away from the sun! Take your time.
